tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Yamaha YZF-R1 is one of the most famous superbikes in the world known for its racing technology and aggressive design.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It features a 998cc inline four engine, advanced electronics, traction control and aerodynamic design.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The R1 is heavily inspired by Yamaha's MotoGP racing technology.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Kawasaki Ninja H2 is one of the most powerful production motorcycles ever built.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It features a supercharged engine, advanced electronics and aerodynamic bodywork.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Ninja H2R track version can exceed 400 km/h.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Ducati Panigale V4 is a premium superbike known for its Italian design and racing performance.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It features a powerful V4 engine, advanced electronics and MotoGP inspired technology.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Panigale V4 replaced the legendary Ducati V-twin superbikes.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Suzuki Hayabusa is one of the most iconic sport bikes known for its speed and unique aerodynamic design.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It features a powerful engine designed for high speed touring and sport riding.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Hayabusa held the title of the fastest production motorcycle in the world for many years.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The BMW S1000RR is a high performance superbike developed by BMW Motorrad.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: It features advanced electronics, quick shifter, traction control and powerful inline four engine.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The S1000RR is widely used in superbike racing competitions.
